title: Operator TUI fullscreen logo splash with compact 8-line status header
purpose: Implement a polished startup effect for the Operator TUI: show the Ananta symbol fullscreen at startup, then shrink/pin it to the top-left as an 8-line compact logo with useful runtime status information next to it.

context.user_goal: Beim Start der TUI soll das Ananta-Symbol anfangs die ganze Seite einnehmen und danach oben links sehr klein weiter angezeigt werden, über 8 Zeilen. Daneben sollen sinnige Statusinformationen stehen, z. B. aktuelles Verzeichnis der TUI und weitere Statusmeldungen.
current_code_state: agent/cli/main.py routes `ananta tui` to client_surfaces.operator_tui.app.main unless --legacy/--fixture is used.
client_surfaces/operator_tui/app.py builds OperatorState and calls InteractiveOperatorTui for real interactive terminal sessions.
client_surfaces/operator_tui/interactive.py uses prompt_toolkit TextArea full_screen=True and re-renders via render_operator_shell().
client_surfaces/operator_tui/renderer.py currently renders a static text shell with title, rule, three columns, status line, command line and hints.

agent/cli/banner.py and agent/cli/assets already provide generated Ananta terminal logo assets; scripts/render_terminal_logo.py can generate ANSI, mono, plain ASCII and ANSI-colored ASCII logo variants.
design_principles: No hand-drawn fallback logo revival. Use SVG-derived generated assets.
Effect must be optional and must not break non-interactive render-once/smoke tests.
Never emit ANSI escape codes into JSON/script output paths.
Use deterministic time/frame behavior testable without real sleeps.
Keep the old renderer path available as a fallback.

code_review_basis.finding: `ananta tui` uses `_run_tui()` and imports `client_surfaces.operator_tui.app.main` for the default Operator TUI path.
Operator TUI currently supports `--render-once`, `--width`, `--height`, `--smoke`, and interactive full-screen mode.
Interactive mode currently has one TextArea and redraws full string output from `render_operator_shell()`.
Current shell renderer has no logo header/splash stage; it starts directly with title/rule and body columns.
Logo generation now supports ASCII-color and edge-aware ASCII assets; suitable for splash and compact logo variants.

milestones.title: Logo asset strategy for splash and compact header

tasks.title: Define TUI logo asset variants and selection rules
tasks.summary: Create a deterministic asset selection layer for fullscreen splash, compact 8-line logo, plain ASCII fallback and no-color fallback.
acceptance_criteria: A module exists that can load logo assets without importing the CLI main module.
Asset lookup supports at least: splash_large, splash_medium, compact_8, compact_8_mono, compact_8_plain_ascii.
Missing asset files degrade gracefully to a built-in compact ASCII fallback, not to a crash.
Asset selection considers terminal width, color support and NO_COLOR.
The implementation does not resurrect the rejected hand-drawn logo drafts.

tasks.title: Generate compact 8-line Ananta logo asset from SVG-derived source
tasks.summary: The pinned header logo must be exactly 8 display lines high and derived from ananta.svg.
acceptance_criteria: A generated compact asset exists with exactly 8 non-empty display lines after stripping ANSI escapes.
The compact asset uses ASCII characters for shape; ANSI color is allowed only in the color variant.
A no-ANSI fallback exists and contains only 7-bit ASCII or explicitly approved mono fallback characters, depending on selected fallback mode.
The generator command used to create the asset is documented in the file header or docs.
Manual visual review confirms the compact logo still resembles the Ananta symbol enough for a header.

tasks.title: Generate fullscreen splash logo variants for different terminal sizes
tasks.summary: Create large startup assets that can fill most of the terminal screen without clipping.
acceptance_criteria: At least three splash sizes exist or can be generated deterministically: wide, medium, narrow.
Splash renderer can center the selected asset vertically and horizontally.
Assets do not contain unbounded trailing whitespace that breaks width calculations.
Color variant uses ANSI TrueColor on ASCII characters; fallback variant has no ANSI escapes.
No generated file under .tmp is tracked.

tasks.title: Implement display-width safe logo measurement helpers
tasks.summary: ANSI-colored assets need correct visible-width measurement and clipping/padding without counting escape sequences.
acceptance_criteria: Helper strips ANSI escape sequences for visible length calculation.
Helper can pad, clip and center ANSI strings without corrupting escape sequences.
Helper treats unknown wide Unicode conservatively or avoids Unicode for ASCII logo modes.
Tests cover plain ASCII, ANSI-colored ASCII and empty lines.
No renderer uses len(raw_ansi_string) for layout decisions where visible width matters.

tasks.title: Create OperatorTuiStatusSnapshot model
tasks.summary: Build a small deterministic status model for the pinned header next to the 8-line logo.
acceptance_criteria: Status snapshot includes cwd, repo root if detectable, relative project path, base endpoint, auth_state, active section, focus pane, mode and current status_message.
Snapshot includes terminal size and graphics mode decision.
Snapshot includes git branch and dirty state when available without slow shell calls in render hot path.
Snapshot collection handles missing git, missing repo and permission errors gracefully.
Sensitive env values such as tokens/passwords are never included.

tasks.title: Add runtime service status lines for useful TUI diagnostics
tasks.summary: Expose meaningful status data next to the logo without overloading the header.
acceptance_criteria: Header status lines can include: cwd, repo, branch, endpoint, auth, section, mode, focus, queue depth, active goals, online agents and last refresh time.
If adapter data is unavailable, the line shows unknown/degraded rather than crashing.
At most 8 status lines are emitted for the 8-line compact header by default.
Long paths are shortened in the middle or made relative to repo root.
Status line content is deterministic enough for snapshot tests when time is injected/frozen.

tasks.title: Render compact logo plus status block as top header
tasks.summary: Replace/augment the current one-line title with an 8-line header: logo left, status right.
acceptance_criteria: render_operator_shell can render an 8-line compact header when enabled.
The compact Ananta logo appears in the top-left area over exactly 8 visible lines.
Status text appears to the right of the logo and does not overlap it.
The body column layout starts below the header and remains visible.
At narrow widths the renderer degrades to a 1-line title/status header or mono compact header.

tasks.title: Keep current bottom status/command/hints lines compatible with new header
tasks.summary: Adding an 8-line header must not break command input, hints and status footer.
acceptance_criteria: Footer status line still shows endpoint/auth/focus/mode/status or an equivalent compact form.
Command line remains visible in command mode.
Hints line remains visible unless terminal height is too small.
Body height calculation subtracts compact header height correctly.
Minimum supported terminal height remains safe with no negative ranges.

tasks.title: Define splash lifecycle states and config
tasks.summary: Create explicit state for startup effect instead of ad-hoc sleeps or implicit rendering hacks.
acceptance_criteria: Splash lifecycle supports states: disabled, fullscreen, transition, compact_header, skipped.
Lifecycle accepts injected monotonic clock for tests.
Config includes enabled, duration_ms, transition_frames, skip_on_keypress and reduced_motion.
Default is enabled only for interactive TTY mode, disabled for render-once/smoke/CI unless explicitly forced.
No real sleeps are used in unit tests.

tasks.title: Implement fullscreen splash renderer
tasks.summary: Render the Ananta logo centered and scaled/selected to occupy most of the terminal page at startup.
acceptance_criteria: Fullscreen splash selects the largest asset that fits width and height.
Splash is centered horizontally and vertically within available terminal size.
Splash includes a minimal loading/status text such as 'starting operator tui...' without hiding the logo.
If no asset fits, it falls back to compact header immediately.
Snapshot tests cover 80x24, 120x32 and 180x48 terminals.

tasks.title: Implement shrink transition frames from fullscreen to compact header
tasks.summary: Create the visible effect that the fullscreen logo reduces into the 8-line top-left header.
acceptance_criteria: Transition consists of deterministic frames selected by elapsed time or frame index.
No frame exceeds terminal width/height.
Transition can be disabled with reduced motion / env flag.
If terminal is too small, transition is skipped and compact header is shown.
Tests verify first frame is splash-like, final frame is compact-header-like.

tasks.title: Allow startup effect to be skipped by keypress or config
tasks.summary: User must never be trapped watching an animation.
acceptance_criteria: Pressing any normal navigation key during splash skips to compact header/main shell.
Command mode is not entered accidentally by the skip key.
ANANTA_TUI_SPLASH=0 disables the splash effect.
NO_COLOR and reduced-motion settings are respected.
Skip behavior is tested without real prompt_toolkit interactive UI.

tasks.title: Add refresh cadence for splash/status header without flicker
tasks.summary: Header status should update when state changes or on refresh, not spam redraw loops.
acceptance_criteria: Header is re-rendered on section switch, refresh, command execution and terminal resize.
No tight timer loop is introduced for static status.
Optional last-refresh timestamp is updated on :refresh.
Interactive TextArea update remains a single full-text replacement unless a richer layout is introduced deliberately.
First-paint measurement remains within existing budget or reports a controlled degraded status.

tasks.title: Add CLI flags and env vars for TUI splash/header behavior
tasks.summary: Expose safe controls for users and CI.
acceptance_criteria: `ananta tui --no-splash` disables fullscreen splash but keeps compact header unless disabled separately.
`ananta tui --no-logo-header` disables compact logo header and uses legacy title line.
`ananta tui --splash-duration-ms <n>` controls startup duration with sane min/max bounds.
Env vars ANANTA_TUI_SPLASH, ANANTA_TUI_LOGO_HEADER and ANANTA_TUI_SPLASH_DURATION_MS are supported.
CLI help documents these flags.

tasks.title: Integrate splash lifecycle into InteractiveOperatorTui startup
tasks.summary: The real interactive TUI should start with fullscreen logo and end in normal shell with compact header.
acceptance_criteria: Interactive startup initially renders fullscreen splash when enabled.
After configured duration/transition, normal operator shell with compact 8-line header is rendered.
Existing keybindings still work after transition.
Non-interactive `--render-once` does not animate unless explicitly requested by a test-only flag.
Errors during splash rendering fall back to normal shell with status_message explaining the fallback.

tasks.title: Add render-once modes for deterministic preview and tests
tasks.summary: Allow developers to inspect splash/header states without launching full interactive TUI.
acceptance_criteria: `ananta tui --render-once --splash-frame fullscreen` prints fullscreen splash preview.
`ananta tui --render-once --splash-frame compact` prints normal shell with compact header.
`ananta tui --render-once --splash-frame transition:<n>` prints a deterministic transition frame if implemented.
Render-once previews respect --width and --height.
These preview flags do not require prompt_toolkit full-screen mode.

tasks.title: Preserve legacy renderer mode as safety fallback
tasks.summary: Do not break existing smoke tests or users who prefer the old compact renderer.
acceptance_criteria: Legacy renderer output can still be selected via flag/env/config.
Existing operator TUI smoke tests remain green or are updated with explicit expected output mode.
The old one-line title path does not import logo asset code unless needed.
If terminal width/height is below minimum, legacy compact rendering is automatically used.
Fallback decision is visible in status_message or debug output.

tasks.title: Add unit tests for logo/status header rendering
tasks.summary: Verify core header behavior without full interactive terminal.
acceptance_criteria: Compact header test verifies exactly 8 visible logo lines in normal terminal size.
Status header contains cwd or repo-relative path.
Status header contains endpoint, auth, section, mode and focus.
ANSI escape sequences do not break visible width assertions.
Narrow terminal tests verify graceful degradation.

tasks.title: Add unit tests for splash lifecycle and transition
tasks.summary: Ensure splash effect is deterministic and safe.
acceptance_criteria: Lifecycle tests use fake clock/frame index and no sleeps.
Fullscreen splash frame contains logo content and not the normal three-column shell body.
Final frame contains compact header plus normal shell body.
Reduced-motion mode skips transition deterministically.
Terminal-too-small case skips splash without crashing.

tasks.title: Add interactive smoke/preview tests for CLI entrypoint
tasks.summary: Validate `ananta tui` flags and render-once previews from the real CLI path.
acceptance_criteria: `ananta tui --render-once --splash-frame fullscreen --width 120 --height 32` exits 0 and prints splash preview.
`ananta tui --render-once --splash-frame compact` exits 0 and prints compact header/status.
`ananta tui --no-splash --render-once` uses compact header without fullscreen preview.
`ANANTA_TUI_SPLASH=0` disables splash in CLI test.
Tests do not require a real TTY.

tasks.title: Document TUI splash/header behavior and tuning
tasks.summary: Explain the new effect, how to disable it and how logo assets are generated.
acceptance_criteria: Docs mention startup fullscreen logo and compact 8-line header.
Docs list CLI flags and env vars for disabling/tuning the effect.
Docs show preview commands for fullscreen and compact render-once modes.
Docs explain that assets are SVG-derived via scripts/render_terminal_logo.py.
README mention stays short and does not embed huge generated logo text.

tasks.title: Run acceptance commands and archive/update TODO status
tasks.summary: Verify implementation and close/move TODO only when all acceptance criteria are met.
acceptance_criteria: Run targeted unit tests for logo layout, status header, splash lifecycle and CLI flags.
Run existing operator TUI smoke tests.
Run manual preview for fullscreen and compact frames at 120x32 and 180x48.
Record test result summary in commit message or TODO status update.
When done, mark all tasks done and move this TODO to todos/archiv or keep active with only remaining tasks.

definition_of_done: Interactive `ananta tui` starts with fullscreen Ananta logo when enabled.
After startup/transition, the logo is pinned top-left as exactly 8 visible lines.
Status information next to the logo includes at minimum cwd/repo path, endpoint, auth state, section, mode, focus and status message.
Effect is disabled/skipped for CI, render-once default or small terminals unless explicitly previewed.
User can disable splash and/or logo header via CLI/env.
Existing TUI navigation and command mode continue to work.
All targeted tests and existing TUI smoke tests pass.
